payload.answer: The proposed 500 one-card-per-row memory write should not be committed automatically. Please choose a repair path: select specific rows to remember, summarize the CSV into durable high-level facts, store it elsewhere as tabular/reference data, or provide criteria for which rows matter.
repair_options: Reject the proposed 500-card atomization and ask the user to identify which specific rows, if any, should become durable memories.
Offer to summarize the CSV into a smaller number of higher-level memory candidates instead of storing one memory per row.
Ask the user whether the CSV is meant to be stored as a reference document, imported into a structured table outside memory, or distilled into durable preferences/facts.
If only some rows contain durable personal facts, request the subset of rows or criteria for selecting them before generating candidate memories.
Keep the large table out of memory and provide a brief explanation that Brain should not commit hundreds of row-level memories without explicit user confirmation.
